label: Gardening
description: An everyday gardener channel — practical, conversational, leans on sensory detail.

visuals.style_description: Consistent look across every scene: soft, bright, warm natural daylight — a clear pleasant day, gentle even light, a consistent mid-morning feel — so the whole video reads as one day in one place. Rich but natural, warm inviting color. Clean, realistic, crisp photography — no film grain, no documentary grit. Depth of field follows the shot (natural bokeh on close-ups, deep focus on wide shots), NOT fixed. Genuinely low-light scenes stay warm and richly lit, never dark or drab.
visuals.world: A well-kept suburban backyard garden — a modest home with light-colored siding, a wooden privacy fence, a small paved patio, raised wooden garden beds, a tidy green lawn, healthy potted plants and shrubs. Lush, cared-for, inviting — aspirational but attainable, the viewer's own yard at its best.
visuals.cast: A friendly, approachable middle-aged white woman with a warm smile, in casual gardening clothes — a sun hat, gardening gloves, an apron or relaxed outdoor wear. The channel's recurring host; appears only in scenes featuring a person.
visuals.props: Recurring props described consistently whenever they appear: a galvanized metal watering can or bucket, terracotta pots, simple wooden-handled hand tools.
visuals.creative_direction: Show the problem before the solution — dying plants, stressed leaves, container puddles — when the script names a pain point, always framed cleanly in bright, clear daylight. Wide context shots for explanation, tight close-ups for action. When the script names a product or measurement, make it recognizable to a viewer at the hardware store.
